title: Pilosella arvicola
taxonomy: Pilosella arvicola is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is classified under the genus Pilosella, which is a group of plants commonly known as 'mouse-ear daisies'. The genus Pilosella is further grouped under the subgenus Pilosella, and the species Pilosella arvicola is classified as a type of plant that is native to certain regions. As a member of the family Asteraceae, Pilosella arvicola is closely related to other plants such as dandelions and sunflowers. The species is also sometimes classified under the genus Senecio, as it was previously known.
uses: Pilosella arvicola is a rare plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in Eastern Europe and Russia. It has been employed to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, colds, and respiratory problems. The plant's leaves and flowers are typically harvested for their medicinal properties, which are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects.
